feed.title: FaultPoint
feed.subtitle: FaultPoint.com is mostly an assortment of vulnerability research and exploit development focused technical blog posts, maintained by elbee. The purpose of this site is to maintain independent research for different corners of RE/VR as I (and other authors) explore them.

entry.title: Designing Low-side Capture Circuits for Power Analysis on Embedded Targets with Internal Regulators

link.title: Designing Low-side Capture Circuits for Power Analysis on Embedded Targets with Internal Regulators

entry.summary: No UFO Board? No problem. A practical guide on creating capture setups for conducting power analysis on arbitrary development boards and embedded targets, as well as overcoming internal regulators and reducing SNR. In this post we'll go over overriding the STM32F401xC's IR for measurements, which does not have much clear public research, to serve as a general guide for building target-focused circuits that make any target work with your chipwhisperer.

entry.title: Designing Low-side Capture Circuits for Power Analysis on Embedded Targets with Internal Regulators

link.title: Designing Low-side Capture Circuits for Power Analysis on Embedded Targets with Internal Regulators

entry.summary: No UFO Board? No problem. A practical guide on creating capture setups for conducting power analysis on arbitrary development boards and embedded targets, as well as overcoming internal regulators and reducing SNR. In this post we'll go over overriding the STM32F401xC's IR for measurements, which does not have much clear public research, to serve as a general guide for building target-focused circuits that make any target work with your chipwhisperer.

entry.title: Ringbus: Deferred Free Across Caches (Writeup)
entry.summary: Writing to modprobe using bad ring buffer handlers. Official writeup for my RSTCON25 challenge.

entry.title: 'You Left this on the Internet?' Finding 8 Zero Days in the WNR854T for DistrictCon Junkyard

link.title: 'You Left this on the Internet?' Finding 8 Zero Days in the WNR854T for DistrictCon Junkyard

entry.summary: How my university club dumpster dived eight CVEs for a year-0 conference including WAN RCE and NVRAM persistence.

entry.title: Tool Release: RopView
entry.summary: A technical post on my new gadget analysis framework.
